lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing there?

lines.speaker: Injured Tortugan
lines.text: I feel... fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a terrible liar, you know? I'll get your bandages sorted as quick as possible. Seaweed and a palm leaf, right?

lines.speaker: Injured Tortugan
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello? Are you okay? That's a pretty nasty looking injury you've got there...

lines.speaker: Injured Tortugan
lines.text: I'm... I'm fine... Please... leave me alone...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Leave you alone? Look at you! You've been badly hurt and need help.

lines.speaker: Injured Tortugan
lines.text: No... No help... I'll fix it myself...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now look, there is no way you're going to be able to fix this on your own. Either you let me help, or you die here. Is that what you want?

lines.speaker: Injured Tortugan
lines.text: No...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then what can I do to help?

lines.speaker: Injured Tortugan
lines.text: Bandages... I was trying to make... bandages. Seaweed and a... a palm leaf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, you take it easy. I'll get your bandages sorted as quick as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're definitely not okay, but I guess I can't force help upon you if you don't want it. I'll be on my way, but please, you really should reconsider.

sections.title: Talking to Injured Tortugan with the makeshift bandages

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: No point wasting time I guess. I'll join you on your boat.

sections.title: Talking to Floopa whilst at sea

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to be going, Floopa?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: You need to head [north/north west/north east/south/south west/south east.

sections.title: Talking to Floopa when close to the island

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to be going, Floopa?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Head to the south of the island. You can dock there.

sections.title: Talking to Floopa near the gangplank

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Right, we're here. I'll meet you ashore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I've got those bandages. Let's get you sorted out...

lines.speaker: Injured Tortugan
lines.text: You... helped me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! Did you expect me to just leave you to die? I'm not a monster!

lines.speaker: Injured Tortugan
lines.text: I'm sorry for not trusting you, it's just... Well, I guess it doesn't matter now. I'm Floopa. Thank you for saving my life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't mention it. I'm [player name]. Pleased to meet you, Floopa.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Thank you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, those bandages should do the job for the time being, but we need to get you some proper help.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Help? No, I'll be fine now. I can sort the rest out myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you? What is your medical background?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Well, I read a book once...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Floopa, I understand that you don't trust me, but I promise I'm only here to help.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: It's not that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What then?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: I'd have to go home...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Home? What's bad about home?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Nothing, but... if I go there, then I'll have failed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Failed at what?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Everything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, those bandages aren't going to last forever, and unfortunately I don't think that book of yours can help... not unless you use the pages as replacement bandages.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: You don't understand!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, Floopa, let me take you home. We can use my boat.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Alright, fine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Floopa. So, where is home?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: The Great Conch, to the south east. I guess I'll join you on your boat.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Hello again, human. If you'd like to check in on Floopa, you'll find her at Elder Raley's home. It's the first one on the right as you head into town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll head right over.

lines.speaker: Mag
lines.text: Hello, human. I'm afraid I can't help you with anything until we've sorted out all the problems that gryphon caused.

lines.speaker: Elder Blunn
lines.text: Sorry, but I can't stop to talk. That gryphon attack has caused more than a few problems that need sorting first.

lines.speaker: Elder Krill
lines.text: Sorry, but I'm too busy to talk right now. I'm still getting things sorted after that gryphon attack.

lines.speaker: Elder Strom
lines.text: No time to talk, human. That gryphon caused no end of problems that need to be dealt with. You'll need to come back later.

lines.speaker: Elder Coco
lines.text: Sorry, human, but I don't have time to talk right now. I need to keep working on sorting out all the problems that gryphon caused.

lines.speaker: Elder Reggle
lines.text: Sorry, human, but I'm not particularly interested in making conversation with those that I don't trust.

sections.title: Trying to use the coral nurseries

lines.speaker: Chet
lines.text: Leave the coral nurseries alone please.

lines.speaker: Chet
lines.text: Hello, human. Please be careful around the coral nurseries.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the west Gryphon caves
Attempting to enter the east Gryphon caves

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Goodness, Floopa, look at you!

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: I'm sorry, Korel. I didn't want to come back, but...

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Nonsense! It would have been foolish to try and deal with this yourself! Now, get yourself over to Elder Raley's at once!

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Ah, human. I take it we have you to thank for getting Floopa to us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right, though she wasn't overly keen on coming.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: That's not surprising, but it's good that she did. She would have been unlikely to survive that injury on her own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think she'll be okay?

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: She will survive. With Elder Raley looking after her, I have no doubt of that. However, it is a nasty wound, and much of the damage will be permanent. Did she say how it happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. She seemed reluctant to tell me much at all, to be honest.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: I see. Well, thank you for helping her, human. Now, I've no doubt you have plenty to be doing. You should be on your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, would it be possible to stay? I'd be keen to make sure she's doing okay.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Don't take this the wrong way, human, but as you may have worked out from Floopa, we tortugans aren't always the biggest fans of company. We generally don't allow humans on the Conch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Generally?

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: There are exceptions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I be an exception?

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: I suppose it is within my right to invite you to remain here for a short time as what we call a tourist. It will allow you to stay on the island while Floopa recovers.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: However, you should be aware that the only way to permanently remain on the Conch is to undergo our Trial by Water and become an honorary tortugan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... Well, let's do this tourist thing for now and we can worry about what happens after that once Floopa is doing better.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Very well. I will see that it is done. Now, if you'd like to check in on Floopa, you'll find her at Elder Raley's home. It's the first one on the right as you head into town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll head right over.

sections.title: Talking to Floopa or Elder Raley

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing, Floopa?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Argh...

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: You might want to leave her to it for a bit. Tidying the wound isn't the most pleasant of experiences.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name]. How are those repairs going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have another list of the work that needs doing?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Don't forget, you'll need a mix of jatoba logs, sea shells and our very own scutes. You'll find some jatoba trees growing to the west of town and the shells are easy enough to find on the various beaches.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: As for the scutes, they get shed quite regularly, so it shouldn't be too hard to find a few dotted around the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Elder Krill
lines.text: You must be the human that Korel spoke of. Welcome to the Conch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you! I hear you have a couple of things in need of repair. I've offered to get them sorted.

lines.speaker: Elder Krill
lines.text: That is very generous of you, human. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Elder Strom
lines.text: Ah, you must be the new tourist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's me! I've offered to help out with some repairs around town.

lines.speaker: Elder Strom
lines.text: Well, in that case, you should get stuck right in.

lines.speaker: Elder Coco
lines.text: Hello there, human. Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm here to help you. I'm sorting out some of the repairs that need doing.

lines.speaker: Elder Coco
lines.text: Oh, I see. That's very generous of you.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Hello again, human. I have finished making arrangements for you to remain on the island as a tourist until Floopa recovers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect! Thank you for sorting that.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: It's not a problem.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: ... really no fuss at all! Anyway, has that potion done anything for you yet?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: I think so. I feel a lot less... like death.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Very good! Very good! A positive first step indeed! We'll give it a bit longer to make sure it properly kicks in, and then we can start on cleaning up that wound.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Oh look, a human! Is this the one you mentioned, Floopa?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Yes, but... [player name], what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, your friend on the docks gave me permission to stick around until you were feeling better.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Oh, thank you, but you really don't need...

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Come now, Floopa. You should be more welcoming to the human.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Of course, you're right. I'm sorry, [player name]. I didn't mean to be rude. This is all just... a lot.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: You can say that again! It looks like you've been in a fight with the Sharhai itself!

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Ah, but you must forgive me, human. Here I am admonishing Floopa for her manners while not showing any myself! I'm Raley, one of the elders here on the Great Conch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's good to meet you. I'm [player name].

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: You'll have to forgive me, but I'm a bit all over the place at the moment. We're still dealing with the aftermath of another blasted gryphon attack!

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: An attack? Was anyone hurt?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: A few cuts and scrapes, but nothing serious. It did manage to make quite the mess around town though. At some point I'll need to help some of the more senior elders with repairs, but one thing at a time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you need help with repairs, maybe I could lend a hand? That way you can focus on looking after Floopa.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: You've helped enough, [player name]. You don't need to trouble yourself further.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: I wouldn't say no to a bit of extra help, though Floopa is right, [player name]. Do not feel like you need to do any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem. What repairs need doing?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Most of the elders will handle their own repair work, so it's just the more senior ones who'll need your help. I've made a list of the work that needs to be done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of materials will I need?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: You'll need a mix of jatoba logs, sea shells and our very own scutes. You'll find some jatoba trees growing to the west of town and the shells are easy enough to find on the various beaches.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: As for the scutes, they get shed quite regularly, so it shouldn't be too hard to find a few dotted around the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! I'll get right to it.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Just come back and let me know if you get stuck with anything. Now, Floopa, I think that potion will have fully done its work now, so let's get to work on that wound.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: This is going to hurt, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Yes, it will probably sting a bit...

lines.speaker: Elder Krill
lines.text: Thank you for helping with those repairs, human. It shouldn't be long now until I can start selling my goods again.

lines.speaker: Elder Strom
lines.text: Not bad on those repairs. Still plenty of things I need to do though. If you need something, you'll have to come back later.

lines.speaker: Elder Coco
lines.text: Hello there, human. Thank you for helping with those repairs. I should hopefully have the stall open again soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think that's everything. I should head back to Raley and Floopa.

sections.title: Talking to Floopa or Elder Raley

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Floopa.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Sorry, [player name], but Raley says I need to get some rest. We'll talk later, once you've sorted out that gryphon.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name]. Any luck with that gryphon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it, but I'm sure I'll have it dealt with soon.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: You should hopefully be able to pick up its trail in the middle of town. Good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good news! The repairs are all sorted.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Wonderful! And, you'll be pleased to know that I was successful in properly tidying up Floopa's wound. I'll need her to stay here for a while to make sure it properly heals, but I expect a full recovery!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's fantastic!

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Is it? Even if I recover, my flipper... it's gone. Without it I'm... useless...

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Floopa, this is a highly traumatic experience you've gone through, and I won't lie to you, it will change your life forever. But Floopa, you are not, and never will be, useless.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: I hope you're right...

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: You should get some rest. We will sort you out with a place to stay here for as long as you need, and I will be on hand to help you however I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm happy to help as well. Is there anything I can do?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: For Floopa? No. Rest is what she now needs. However, and please do say no if it's too much to ask, you might be able to help us out some more with that gryphon problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Unfortunately, while we were able to injure it, the gryphon managed to escape before we could finish it off. One that aggressive can't be allowed to return.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Korel was planning to deal with it himself, but other matters have required his attention.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I can track it down.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Very good! You really are too kind! It seemed like the injury prevented the gryphon from flying, so you hopefully won't have much trouble picking up its trail. I suggest you start in the middle of town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do. I'm sure I'll have it dealt with in no time!

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Very good!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are humans generally not allowed here?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Oh, [player name], I hope you haven't taken offense! It's really nothing personal. It's just how we do things.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: It's not just limited to humans either. You might have noticed that there are no adult tortugans here on the Conch, just juveniles and elders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it's a societal thing?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Yes, I suppose it is. We tortugans like our privacy, and we don't like being overly dependent on others. It is these preferences that have formed our way of life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I guess that gives me another question then. If the adults aren't here, where are they?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Out in the wider world, living their lives. They return here from time to time, usually when it is time to mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, so that explains why Floopa was reluctant to return here.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Yes, there can be a certain level of... pride, shall we say, amongst the younger adults. They have been known to see independence as a constant requirement.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: I can hear you, you know?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: It's rude to eavesdrop, Floopa! You should be resting!

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Anyway, as I was saying, it is a trait seen amongst many of the younger adults. They soon grow out of it. No one wants to be a burden of course, but we elders understand that there is no shame in asking for help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds like a good balance.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Indeed it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are gryphons a common problem for your people?

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Unfortunately so. They are the main predator we have to deal with here on the Conch, and it seems they have decided that we are quite the delicacy.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Thankfully, most of them keep to the quieter parts of the island, making them easier to handle. However, from time to time, one will grow bold enough to try and target the town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry. It can't be nice living with that threat.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Ah, it is nothing to be sad about. We have grown used to it. Besides, while the gryphons may prey upon us, in many ways, we prey upon them as well.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Killing them keeps their numbers under control, and their claws and feathers come in quite useful. In fact, all tortugans must obtain a gryphon claw as part of their Trial by Water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Trial by Water? Korel mentioned that as well.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: It is a rite of passage that all juvenile tortugans must go through on their journey to adulthood. On rare occasions, members of other races have undergone the Trial themselves, becoming honourary tortugans.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Perhaps at some point you will be able to take on the trial yourself. You have already shown yourself to be both capable and trustworthy. However, that is a conversation for another day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get to it.

sections.title: Talking to Elder Korel, Elder Raley, or Floopa

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Quickly, human! You need to sail to the Little Pearl, south east of the Conch. I will meet you there. Oh, and don't forget to pick up a shield from Elder Blunn outside!

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Hurry, [player name]! You need to head to the Little Pearl!

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: You need to get to the Pearl, [player name]! We can't lose those eggs!

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: ... holding it at bay, but they need support quick!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Another gryphon attack, but this time on the Little Pearl!

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: This doesn't make sense! They've never attacked the Pearl before!

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: I'm heading over right away. The eggs must be protected!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do?

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Join me, human! Sail to the Little Pearl as fast as you can and help protect our eggs!

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: You'll find the Pearl south east of the Conch. It shouldn't take you long to get there, but make sure you don't go underprepared! It sounds like this gryphon is one of the larger ones.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Yes, make sure you speak to Elder Blunn outside first. He can sort you out with a suitable shield.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll grab one of those shields and set off right away!

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: I will meet you there, human.

sections.title: Talking to Elder Blunn after failing to take the shield

lines.speaker: Elder Blunn
lines.text: Hello again, human. I assume you're here for that shield?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Elder Blunn
lines.text: Do not lose it, for I won't be able to give you another for free. Any more will come at a cost.

lines.speaker: Elder Blunn
lines.text: Hello, human. Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a shield to protect me against one of the larger gryphons. I'm afraid it's quite urgent.

lines.speaker: Elder Blunn
lines.text: Ah, now that is something I can sort for you. I started making these at the suggestion of a human tourist. They're fashioned in the style of our own shells.

lines.speaker: Elder Blunn
lines.text: We use them alongside our spears, but for a being of your size, wearing them on your back would work best.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like my very own shell?

lines.speaker: Elder Blunn
lines.text: Precisely! Normally I would charge for one of these, but you claim the situation is urgent, and I trust you are not lying. As such, I will grant you one for free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Elder Blunn
lines.text: Do not lose it, human, for I won't be able to give you another for free. Any more will come at a cost.

sections.title: Upon arriving at the Little Pearl

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's all that about?

lines.speaker: [Crewmate]
lines.text: Er... Problem over here, Captain!

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Excellent work! The beast is well and truly dead!

lines.speaker: Elder Hubber
lines.text: Thank you for your help with that gryphon, human.

lines.speaker: Elder Root
lines.text: The eggs are safe once more! Good work, human.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: You should return to Raley and Floopa on the Conch, human. Tell them what you told me about that ship with the red sails. See if they can make any sense of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll head back right away.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Very good work, human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are the eggs okay?

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: As good as can be expected. We lost a fair few, but it could have been much worse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry about the ones that were lost.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Even without a gryphon involved, not all eggs make it. It is an unfortunate reality of life. You need not apologise.

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: Now, I need to get to work on finding out how this happened. To our knowledge, no gryphon has ever travelled this far from the Conch before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that... It may be nothing, but when I arrived here, I saw a ship with red sails sailing away from the island. You don't think it's connected, do you?

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: A ship with red sails? Curious... and concerning... Hmm...

lines.speaker: Elder Korel
lines.text: You should return to Raley and Floopa on the Conch, human. Tell them what you have told me. See if they can make any sense of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll head back right away.

sections.title: Talking to Floopa or Elder Raley

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: You're back, [player name]! What news from the Pearl?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The gryphon has been dealt with. The eggs are safe.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Fantastic news!

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Did you see anything that might explain why the gryphon attacked?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Possibly, but it's hard to say for sure. There was a ship near the island. It had red sails.

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: Red sails? Surely not...

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: What is it, Floopa?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: It's... I'm not sure. It might... It might have something to do with my injury.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: My captain... My crew... They betrayed me. Stabbed me with my own spear and abandoned me to the waves...

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: Floopa... I'm so sorry...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This ship? Does it have something to do with your old crew?

lines.speaker: Floopa
lines.text: I think so... but I need time to think... I need to work out what it all means...

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: More than that! You need more rest as well. [player name], we should give her the time she needs. Return to us later once she has rested.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: In the meantime, may I suggest you take this opportunity to further explore our island. I'm sure you'll find it has a lot to offer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might just do that. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Elder Raley
lines.text: No, no, no! Thank you, [player name]! We will talk again soon.
